title: Cantharospermum scarabaeoides
intro: Cantharospermum scarabaeoides, commonly known as the "Scarab seed," is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, characterized by its unique seed pods resembling the elytra of a beetle. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, pain, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Cantharospermum scarabaeoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, herbal preparations made from the plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory issues to skin conditions.
